metadata.description: Maps each deduplicated threat to its discovering Phase B specialists for confidence analysis

TECH-H001.title: VoteWeighting.removeNominee Corrupts Global pointsSum Slope, Distorting All Emission Distributions
TECH-H001.duplicate_group_description: VoteWeighting.removeNominee fails to subtract the removed nominee's slope from pointsSum[nextTime].slope, causing the global weight sum to decay at an incorrect rate and distorting all nomineeRelativeWeight calculations

TECH-H007.title: VoteWeighting Removed Nominee's changesSum Entries Persist and Distort Future Slope Adjustments
TECH-H007.duplicate_group_description: VoteWeighting.removeNominee does not clean up future changesSum/changesWeight entries for voters who directed votes to the removed nominee, causing ongoing slope distortion at lock expiration times

TECH-H005.title: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit Hard Reverts on Any Invalid Target, Blocking Entire Batch
TECH-H005.duplicate_group_description: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit hard-reverts with TargetEmissionsZero if any single target in a batch has zero emissions limit, blocking all L1 staking targets in the same batch — unlike L2 dispensers which gracefully withhold and continue

TECH-H003.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem Bypasses Staking Target Re-verification
TECH-H003.duplicate_group_description: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem() does not re-verify staking target validity via stakingFactory.verifyInstanceAndGetEmissionsAmount, allowing queued deposits to flow to targets that became invalid after queuing (TOCTOU)

TECH-H004.title: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking Uses Wrong Address in Error Message (depository instead of dispenser)
TECH-H004.duplicate_group_description: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking() checks msg.sender against dispenser but error message incorrectly reports depository address: revert ManagerOnly(msg.sender, depository)

TECH-H009.title: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives Reverts When Reward and TopUp Are Both Zero
TECH-H009.duplicate_group_description: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives() reverts with ClaimIncentivesFailed when both reward and topUp are zero because the success variable is never set to true in the zero-incentive path

TECH-H010.title: StakingFactory Owner Cannot Disable Malicious Staking Instances Created by Untrusted Deployers
TECH-H010.duplicate_group_description: StakingFactory.setInstanceStatus() restricts enable/disable to original deployer only, with no factory owner override, preventing emergency disablement of malicious instances

TECH-H012.title: Donation Amount Truncation in _trackServiceDonations Creates Undistributed ETH
TECH-H012.duplicate_group_description: Tokenomics._trackServiceDonations truncates donation amounts via uint96(amounts[i] / numServiceUnits), losing the remainder which is still counted in totalDonationsETH but never distributed to units

ECON-H008.title: StakingBase Checkpoint Reward Rounding Systematically Favors First Eligible Service in Array
ECON-H008.duplicate_group_description: StakingBase checkpoint reward rounding dust is systematically assigned to the first eligible service (index 0) rather than distributed fairly

TECH-H006.title: Tokenomics._finalizeIncentivesForUnitId Silent uint96 Truncation on Reward and TopUp Accumulation
TECH-H006.duplicate_group_description: Tokenomics._finalizeIncentivesForUnitId casts accumulated uint256 totalIncentives to uint96 without overflow checking, silently truncating large accumulated rewards/topUps

TECH-H013.title: Permissionless Tokenomics initialization enables permanent takeover if deployed/used as standalone implementation
TECH-H013.duplicate_group_description: Tokenomics.initializeTokenomics() is external with only owner==address(0) guard, enabling front-running takeover if deployed without atomic initialization

TECH-H014.title: Permissionless staking implementation initialization allows attacker-controlled staking instances with arbitrary parameters
TECH-H014.duplicate_group_description: StakingToken/StakingNativeToken initialize() functions are external with only single-init guard, enabling attacker initialization if deployed outside StakingFactory's atomic workflow

TECH-H015.title: Dispenser claimStakingIncentives can transfer/mint OLAS to a zero or malicious depositProcessor if chainId mapping is unset or set to non-contract
TECH-H015.duplicate_group_description: Dispenser does not validate mapChainIdDepositProcessors[chainId] is non-zero before transferring OLAS and calling sendMessage, risking token burns or DoS when chain mapping is unset

ECON-H013.title: Array Out-of-Bounds DoS in unstake Due to Stale Index After Eviction
ECON-H013.duplicate_group_description: StakingBase.unstake() uses a stale array index from before checkpoint() evictions, potentially causing out-of-bounds access or incorrect element removal in setServiceIds

TECH2-H002.title: Asymmetric `effectiveBond` Adjustment Inflates Bond Capacity During Year Changes
TECH2-H002.duplicate_group_description: Tokenomics.checkpoint() effectiveBond adjustment only handles the case where actual maxBond exceeds estimate, missing the downward adjustment when inflation drops at year boundaries

TECH2-H006.title: Tokenomics.trackServiceDonations() does not validate `amounts.length == serviceIds.length`, enabling treasury-originated misaccounting or unexpected reverts inside _trackServiceDonations
TECH2-H006.duplicate_group_description: VoteWeighting._getSum()/_getWeight() loop bounded by MAX_NUM_WEEKS (53) fails to advance timeSum/timeWeight if not checkpointed for >53 weeks, causing permanent state stagnation

OPS-H013.title: Permissionless VoteWeighting checkpointing can be used to force-write gauge state and influence downstream incentive calculations
OPS-H013.duplicate_group_description: Permissionless calls to VoteWeighting.checkpointNominee() and Dispenser.calculateStakingIncentives() allow anyone to force-write gauge weight state, potentially influencing timing of weight updates

TECH-H002.title: L2 Target Dispenser Batch Processing DoS via Malicious Staking Contract deposit() Revert

TECH-H008.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint() Lacks Reentrancy Guard Despite External Call to Treasury Before Epoch Increment

TECH-H011.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2 Leaves Dangling ERC20 Approval After Failed deposit() During _processData

TECH-H016.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint lacks reentrancy guard around external treasury.rebalanceTreasury call, enabling epoch state corruption if treasury is malicious/compromised

TECH-H017.title: L2 queued staking deposits can bypass later factory disablement because redeem() does not re-verify target emissions limit

TECH-H018.title: StakingBase.unstake may allow unstaking even if service was evicted in same checkpoint call due to index-based membership scan ambiguity

ECON-H001.title: VoteWeighting.removeNominee Fails to Update pointsSum Slope, Corrupting Global Emission Distribution

ECON-H002.title: VoteWeighting.removeNominee Leaves Stale changesSum/changesWeight Entries Causing Ongoing Weight Sum Corruption

ECON-H003.title: EthereumDepositProcessor Hard Reverts on Any Invalid Target, Blocking All L1 Staking Incentive Claims in Batch

ECON-H004.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem Bypasses Staking Target Verification, Depositing to Potentially Invalid Contracts

ECON-H005.title: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking Uses Wrong Address in Error Message, Potentially Masking Unauthorized Callers

ECON-H006.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint Lacks Reentrancy Guard Despite External Call to Treasury Before Epoch State Update

ECON-H007.title: Permissionless Nominee Addition Enables L1 Batch Claim Griefing via Unverified Staking Targets

ECON-H009.title: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives Reverts When Zero Incentives Accumulated Instead of Returning Gracefully

ECON-H010.title: Tokenomics Donation Amount Truncation Causes Per-Unit Reward Loss While Epoch Total Remains Inflated

ECON-H011.title: Wormhole Bridge Precision Loss Causes Permanent L2 Staking Queue Lockup

ECON-H012.title: removeNominee Corrupts Global Weight Slope Causing Total Emission Distribution Failure

ECON-H014.title: L1 Staking Batch DoS via Single Invalid Target Revert

ECON-H015.title: Queued L2 Staking Redemptions Bypass Current Emission Limits

ECON-H016.title: Systematic Reward Dust Extraction via Staking Array Ordering

OPS-H001.title: VoteWeighting.removeNominee Corrupts pointsSum Slope, Inflating All Remaining Nominees' Relative Weights

OPS-H002.title: Unrevoked Votes on Removed Nominees Leave Residual changesSum Entries That Further Distort Global Weight Decay

OPS-H003.title: EthereumDepositProcessor._deposit Hard Reverts Entire Batch If Any Single Target Has Zero Emissions

OPS-H004.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2.redeem Deposits OLAS to Staking Targets Without Re-Verifying Current Validity

OPS-H005.title: Dispenser.retain() Callable During Staking Incentive Pause, Advancing Retainer Epoch Counter and Modifying Staking Inflation

OPS-H006.title: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking Error Message References Wrong Manager Address (depository Instead of dispenser)

OPS-H007.title: Dispenser.claimOwnerIncentives Reverts Instead of Returning Zero When No Incentives Are Available

OPS-H008.title: Silent uint96 Truncation in Tokenomics._finalizeIncentivesForUnitId Can Cause Permanent Reward/TopUp Loss

OPS-H009.title: Tokenomics.changeTokenomicsParameters Uses Unsafe uint72/uint32 Casts Enabling Silent Parameter Truncation

OPS-H010.title: StakingFactory Owner Cannot Disable Malicious Staking Instances; Only Original Deployer Has Toggle Authority

OPS-H011.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint() Lacks Reentrancy Guard Despite External Call to Treasury Before Epoch Counter Increment

OPS-H012.title: Unrestricted nominee creation enables attacker-controlled staking target to siphon cross-chain staking emissions

OPS-H014.title: Tokenomics epoch settlement can be re-entered via Treasury.rebalanceTreasury to corrupt epochCounter and incentive accounting

OPS-H015.title: Tokenomics.refundFromStaking has incorrect ManagerOnly error argument, masking unauthorized-caller incidents and complicating incident response

OPS-H016.title: L1 deposit processors can be permanently bricked or misdirected by one-time L2 target dispenser misconfiguration (owner becomes address(0))

OPS-H017.title: L2 target dispenser redeem path can bypass updated staking target validity because it does not re-verify against factory limits

OPS-H018.title: Any address can call StakingToken.initialize / StakingNativeToken.initialize first on a freshly deployed implementation, permanently capturing configuration

OPS-H019.title: ActivityChecker staticcall failure defaults to 'inactive', enabling griefing/DoS on reward distribution and evictions if checker is fragile

TECH2-H001.title: Unremoved Slope in `removeNominee` Corrupts Global Weight Sum

TECH2-H003.title: Queued Staking Deposits Bypass Emissions Limit Verification on Redemption

TECH2-H004.title: `removeNominee` Fails to Clear `mapNomineeIds` if Nominee is Last Element

TECH2-H005.title: Native ETH Permanently Locked if `migrate` is Called Before `drain`

TECH2-H007.title: Potential Reentrancy in `checkpoint` via Treasury Rebalance

TECH2-H008.title: StakingToken.deposit() updates accounting before transferFrom, enabling balance/availableRewards inflation if stakingToken is non-standard (reentrant or returns true but transfers less)

TECH2-H009.title: StakingNativeToken._withdraw() can be weaponized for DoS by multisig recipients that intentionally revert on receiving ETH

TECH2-H010.title: Dispenser._distributeStakingIncentives() and batch variant do not validate depositProcessor != address(0), risking token burns/DoS if chain mapping unset

ECON2-H004.title: VoteWeighting Checkpoint Loop Fails to Advance Time When Exceeding MAX_NUM_WEEKS

ECON2-H007.title: Queued Staking Deposits Bypass Re-verification of Target Validity

ECON2-H008.title: Service Donation Truncation Causes Permanent ETH Lock in Tokenomics

ECON2-H009.title: VoteWeighting changesSum Underflow DoS After Nominee Removal

ECON2-H010.title: Dispenser retain() Uses Stale Retainer Weight Due to Missing Checkpoint

ECON2-H012.title: Tokenomics accountOwnerIncentives Silent uint96 Truncation on Accumulated Rewards

ECON2-H014.title: Dispenser removeNominee Epoch Timing Can Block Last-Epoch Incentive Claims

ECON2-H016.title: Tokenomics changeIncentiveFractions Allows Zero Staking Parameters Propagation

ECON2-H017.title: DefaultTargetDispenserL2 processDataMaintenance Allows Owner to Bypass Staking Verification

OPS2-H001.title: Global Weight Sum Corruption via Nominee Removal

OPS2-H002.title: L1 Staking Incentive Batch DoS via Disabled Target

OPS2-H003.title: Queued Redemptions Bypass Emissions Limit Verification

OPS2-H004.title: Factory Owner Lacks Authority to Disable Malicious Staking Instances

OPS2-H005.title: Public Staking Calculation Function Modifies VoteWeighting State

OPS2-H006.title: Donation Amount Truncation Causes Reward Discrepancy

OPS2-H007.title: Unrevoked Votes on Removed Nominees Distort Global Weight Sum

OPS2-H008.title: Permanent Tokenomics Freeze via Prolonged Checkpoint Delay

OPS2-H009.title: Unprivileged caller can add arbitrary nominees to VoteWeighting, forcing Dispenser.addNominee() and potentially freezing staking incentive claiming via mass nominee spam

OPS2-H010.title: Permissionless StakingFactory allows attacker to deploy abusive staking instances and self-disable/enable them to disrupt L1/Ethereum batch deposits (hard revert) and increase withheld/queue pressure on L2

OPS2-H011.title: Any external account can call Tokenomics.initializeTokenomics() on the implementation contract, seizing ownership and setting critical managers if not deployed behind a guarded proxy

OPS2-H012.title: StakingToken/StakingNativeToken proxy instances can be initialized by anyone if deployed outside StakingFactory (or if initPayload is attacker-controlled), enabling hostile configuration and asset capture

OPS2-H013.title: Tokenomics.checkpoint() can be re-entered via Treasury.rebalanceTreasury(), potentially corrupting epoch transitions and breaking epoch monotonicity

OPS2-H014.title: Unbounded call cost in Tokenomics.trackServiceDonations() (external registry queries per service/unit) enables griefing against Treasury-triggered donation tracking by choosing services with extreme unit counts
